Make devg list fast, move stats behind --stats flag

Also fix clippy collapsible_if in remote start.

commit.message:
feat: v1.0.9 — Agent Drift Monitor (behavioral degradation detection)

Implements a lightweight subset of the Agent Stability Index (ASI) from
Rath arXiv:2601.04170 "Agent Drift" (2026).

New components:
- `src/tmux_orchestrator/drift_monitor.py`: DriftMonitor class that
  polls agent panes and publishes `agent_drift_warning` STATUS events
  when the composite drift score falls below the configured threshold.
  Three sub-scores (role/idle/length) with weights 0.50/0.30/0.20.
- `tests/test_drift_monitor.py`: 34 unit and integration tests.
- `schemas.py`: DriftWarningPayload model for agent_drift_warning events.
- `config.py`: drift_threshold, drift_idle_threshold, drift_monitor_poll.
- `orchestrator.py`: DriftMonitor start/stop integration;
  get_agent_drift_stats() and all_agent_drift_stats() accessors.
- `web/app.py`: GET /agents/{id}/drift and GET /drift endpoints.
- OpenAPI snapshot regenerated.

Reference: Rath arXiv:2601.04170 (2026); DESIGN.md §10.20 (v1.0.9).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove ticket_number from explicit selects (column not in DB)

Migration 002 was never applied so hiring_requests.ticket_number does
not exist. PostgREST silently returned 0 rows when the column was named
in an explicit select, causing the candidate detail page to show empty
applications. Removed ticket_number from the two routes that referenced
it directly; code that uses select('*') is unaffected.

Add single-page HireWizard — four-step wizard component

Replaces multi-route hire flow with a self-contained wizard rendered at
/org/hire/define. Steps: ① Upload Brief (textarea + structured fields +
drag-and-drop) › ② Matched Twyns (2-col grid + type filters + hearts) ›
③ Upskill (Expertise / Integrations / Personification tabs + live cost bar)
› ④ Interview (chat + prompt chips + star rating + notes). Step navigator
uses numbered circles, chevron separators, and active purple underline bar.
Removes HireStepIndicator from hire layout — wizard owns its own nav.

feat: browser tab, DHCP simulation, and persistent SSH sessions

- Browser tab on PC/Laptop/Server nodes renders web server pageContent
  in a sandboxed <iframe srcdoc>, resolves hostnames via local DNS records,
  animates DNS + HTTP packets, supports back/forward history and error page
- DHCP server toggle on Router config (KS4+) with configurable pool range;
  clients run dhclient or ipconfig /renew to trigger the 4-way handshake
  (DISCOVER → OFFER → REQUEST → ACK) with UDP packet animation; IP, gateway
  and subnet are live-updated on the node
- SSH sessions persist until 'exit' is typed; title bar and prompt update to
  show the remote host; commands run in the context of the remote node
- Home Network and School Network presets ship with DHCP pre-enabled

commit.message:
City-alive LIVE: two-layer feed with real venues

STRUCTURAL vs LOCAL signal layers:
- VAGUES MAJEURES: concerts, airports, arenas, expos
- OPPORTUNITÉS LOCALES: restaurants, clubs, theatres

Signal cards now show:
- Category icons (🎵🍽🎭✈🍸🚆🎪🎉🏨)
- Venue name prominent (not buried in reason)
- Cause line (Sortie concert, Fermeture restaurants)
- Intensity dots visualization

New helpers:
- getSignalCategory() for icon mapping
- isStructuralSignal() for layer classification
- extractVenueName() for prominent display
- generateCauseLine() for concrete reasons

Drivers now see the city moving, not abstract flux directions.

commit.message:
feat(compiler): cross-file reactivity — Layer 1 + Layer 2a (#995)

* plan: cross-file reactivity analysis design doc (#987)

Design doc for fixing callback/thunk classification (Layer 1) and
cross-file reactivity manifest system (Layer 2). Includes adversarial
reviews from josh (DX), pm (scope), and ben (technical feasibility).

All 10 blocking review items resolved. CTO approved 2026-03-07.

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): skip function defs in computed classification (#992)

* fix(compiler): skip function defs in computed classification [#988]

Arrow functions and function expressions are stable references — they
should never be wrapped in computed(). The initializer's top-level AST
node kind (ArrowFunction/FunctionExpression) is checked, and such
consts are excluded from the computed classification loop.

Updates 2 existing tests and adds 4 new tests: function expression,
arrow thunk capturing let signal, IIFE (still computed), and a
regression guard for value expressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(compiler): add e2e tests for callback classification [#988]

Adds 3 integration tests that verify the full compiler pipeline
(analyzer → transformers → output) correctly handles function
definitions:
- Arrow function referencing signal → NOT wrapped in computed()
- Arrow function referencing query signal property → NOT computed()
- Function expression referencing signal → NOT computed()

Each test also verifies that value expressions in the same component
are still correctly wrapped in computed() (regression guard).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(compiler): replace hardcoded registry with framework manifest [#989] (#994)

Introduces a ReactivityManifest system that replaces the hardcoded
SIGNAL_API_REGISTRY for classifying signal-returning APIs.

Changes:
- Add ReactivityManifest types (ReactivityManifest, LoadedReactivityManifest,
  ExportReactivityInfo, ReactivityShape) to types.ts
- Create packages/ui/reactivity.json with framework manifest for query(),
  form(), createLoader(), useContext(), signal()
- Create reactivity-manifest.ts with loadManifestFromJson() and
  loadFrameworkManifest() utilities (JSON arrays → Set<string> conversion)
- Add CompileOptions.manifests parameter for passing pre-loaded manifests
- Refactor buildImportAliasMap to use manifests when provided, falling
  back to hardcoded registry for backward compatibility
- Add resolveSignalApiConfig helper that checks manifest first, then
  hardcoded registry

The hardcoded registry is kept as fallback for callers that don't
provide manifests (e.g., existing tests). Layer 2b will wire the
Bun plugin to auto-load manifests.

10 new manifest tests + 1 new integration test. All 436 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): unwrap type wrappers in isFunctionDef check [#988] (#996)

The isFunctionDef check only inspected the direct initializer node kind,
missing arrow functions wrapped in ParenthesizedExpression, AsExpression,
SatisfiesExpression, or TypeAssertion. Adds unwrapTypeWrappers() helper
that peels through these wrappers before checking for ArrowFunction or
FunctionExpression.

Found by adversarial review on PR #992.

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): address manifest review issues (#997)

* fix(compiler): address manifest review issues [#989]

- Export manifest types and functions from public API (index.ts)
- Add console.warn for unsupported manifest versions per design doc 2.2.1
- Auto-load framework manifest for @vertz/ui when no explicit manifest provided
- Remove dead code (hardcoded registry fallback in buildImportAliasMap)
- Add integration test verifying auto-load behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): use require.resolve for framework manifest path [#989]

- Replace __dirname-based path resolution with require.resolve('@vertz/ui/reactivity.json')
  so the path survives bundling and works in published packages
- Add reactivity.json to @vertz/ui files and exports map
- Remove dead hardcoded registry fallback (getSignalApiConfig) from resolveSignalApiConfig
- Remove unused node:path import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Extract shared React components and contexts into scribe-react-common (#61)

* Refactor scribe-react into three packages to fix test segfaults

Break scribe-react into smaller packages so the test suite no longer
segfaults from running too many tests in a single process:

- scribe-react-common: shared contexts, hooks, utils, UI components,
  and test infrastructure (54 tests)
- scribe-react-note: note editor/viewer, drafts, version footer,
  conflict warning (12 tests)
- scribe-react: app shell, routing, remaining pages and tests

Also updates CI with path filters and test jobs for each new package,
and adds Makefile dependency targets with correct ordering.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012YF68wXkb7cAvBySFVXB1y

* Move code from scribe-react to scribe-react-common and scribe-react-note

Delete source files and tests from scribe-react that were already copied
into the new packages, and update all remaining imports to reference
scribe-react-common and scribe-react-note via their package names.

Moved to scribe-react-common:
- contexts (tributary, syncStatus, bottomNav)
- hooks (useOnlineStatus, useDocumentTitle)
- utils (crypto, links, markdown) and their tests
- components (Breadcrumbs, Layout, MoveModal, SearchBar, etc.)
- UI primitives (Badge, Button, Card, Input, Navbar)
- test infrastructure (setup, test-server, test-utils)
- library-creation and integration tests

Moved to scribe-react-note:
- EditorPage, NoteViewPage
- VersionFooter, ConflictWarning
- draftStorage, useDraftAutoSave, saveNote
- saveNote, useDraftAutoSave, ConflictWarning, VersionFooter tests

Also adds React/react-dom/react-router deduplication aliases in
vitest.config.ts to prevent duplicate module instances when importing
components from sub-packages.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012YF68wXkb7cAvBySFVXB1y

* Remove React deduplication aliases (handled by npm workspaces)

With npm workspaces, all packages share a single copy of react,
react-dom, and react-router from the root node_modules. The explicit
path.resolve aliases in vitest.config.ts were needed for the old
file: dependency approach but now break resolution since there's no
local node_modules/react in each workspace package.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012YF68wXkb7cAvBySFVXB1y

* Deduplicate test infrastructure into scribe-react-common

Remove duplicated setup.ts, test-server.ts, and test-utils.ts from
scribe-react and scribe-react-note. Both packages now import test
infra from scribe-react-common, which is the canonical location.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012YF68wXkb7cAvBySFVXB1y

* Fix Tailwind content scanning for moved components

Add scribe-react-common and scribe-react-note source directories to
Tailwind's content array so utility classes used in components that
were moved to those packages (Layout, Breadcrumbs, MoveModal, etc.)
are included in the generated CSS.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012YF68wXkb7cAvBySFVXB1y

---------

commit.message:
feat(connect): add AlchemyConnect — universal tunnel/bus for external apps

WebSocket transport layer at /ws/connect with QR-locked device pairing,
agent-based message routing, offline queue, and keepalive. First agent:
ChatAgent for local LLM chat via SmartRouter/Ollama.

- Protocol: AlchemyMessage envelope with agent field routing
- Pairing: QR code generation, SQLite token registry, device management
- Hub: auth, routing, push/broadcast, ping, connection lifecycle
- Queue: SQLite-backed offline messages, drain on reconnect
- ConnectAgent ABC: extensible agent system for phone-callable endpoints
- REST: /v1/connect/pair/qr, /devices, /status
- 49 tests passing

commit.message:
feat: split self-setup from bootstrap.json into config.json

Move engine self-setup declarations (tools, path_entries, venv) from
bootstrap.json into config.json under a new self_setup key. Bootstrap's
bootstrap.json becomes a normal manifest processed in step 4 — no
special treatment, no bootstrap: false.

- Add self_setup to defaults/config.json, bump schema to v5
- Add v4→v5 migration with hardcoded fallback
- New _process_self_setup() processes only tools/path/venv
- Enforce step 4 processing order: bootstrap first, same-marketplace
  next, others last
- Remove bootstrap: false from bootstrap.json and unreal-kit
- Remove step 3d (no_bootstrap auto-population)
- Fix Windows test failures: path separators, shell quoting, HOME vs
  USERPROFILE, flat vs nested JSON test data, hardcoded /usr/bin
- New test_self_setup.py with 6 tests

217 passed, 2 skipped (cross-marketplace subprocess tests on Windows)

commit.message:
Add lite RAG engine for ad-hoc queries

New components:
- src/rag/document_store.py: Loads and chunks markdown documents
- src/rag/retriever.py: TF-IDF based document retrieval
- src/rag/rag_engine.py: Query processing and response generation

Features:
- Indexes 41 documents into 1055 searchable chunks
- Falls back to RAG when rule-based queries return poor results
- Supports ad-hoc questions like "how were risks mitigated"
- Returns relevant excerpts with source citations

Integration:
- Chat engine now uses RAG as fallback for low-confidence responses
- RAG initializes automatically on server startup

AI-ready Bible dataset and knowledge graph. KJV public domain text
with 31,100 verses across 1,189 chapters, cross-reference graph

- Passage chunking (verse/passage/chapter)
- Cross-reference graph builder
- Scripture entity extraction (Echology pattern)
- Embedding generation (Vanta integration)
- Interactive Flask explorer app
- Full documentation: architecture, schema, roadmap
- Echology case study and founder integration guide

perf(bundle): S2 code-splitting — socket.io, debug-generator, recharts lazy

- S2-1: Convert 5 socket.io-client static imports to dynamic import()
  (useRealtimeConflicts, useContextualMessagesWebSocket, websocket/client,
  RuleNotificationService, useNotificationsWebSocket) — saves ~100KB
  since WEBSOCKET_ENABLED=false, the import never executes

- S2-2: Code-split debug-generator page via next/dynamic thin shell
  (830KB of ValidationRulesService, StaffingPlanService, DiagnosticAnalyzer
  moved to separate chunk, loaded only on /admin/debug-generator)

- S2-3: Add 6 lazy chart exports to LazyCharts.tsx (EquityChart,
  AssignmentDistribution, RuleDashboardPage, PredictiveMaintenanceDashboard,
  PerformanceDashboard, FatigueDashboard) — recharts ~864KB deferred

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(security): harden file uploads — magic bytes, multer limits, path traversal

- Add magic bytes validation utility (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP/PDF/ZIP signatures)
- Validate file content matches claimed MIME type before upload
- Add multer fileSize limits to all FileInterceptor usages:
  - storage: 100MB, complaints (public): 10MB, data-parser: 10MB
- Sanitize folder path in storage controller + service (prevent ../ traversal)
- Sanitize download filename in presigned download (prevent header injection)
- Block dangerous MIME types in base64 upload (exe, shellscript, html, js)

Critical fix for public complaint endpoint — unauthenticated users could
previously upload arbitrary files by spoofing Content-Type header.

commit.message:
Fix AccuracyCoin P19 Sprites On Scanline 0: secondary OAM + per-dot sprite evaluation FSM

Add secondary OAM buffer (32 bytes) and per-dot sprite evaluation state machine:
- Dots 1-64: clear secondary OAM, dots 65-256: evaluate sprites per-dot
- ppu_r_2004() returns oamCopyBuffer during rendering for accurate $2004 reads
- Pre-render line dot 257: compute sprite data for scanline 0 using (261&255)=5
- Scanline 0 uses pre-render sprite data for sprite 0 hit detection

blargg: 174/174, AccuracyCoin: 119→120/136 (+1)
